zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel FitzJohn於大約1233年出生在英國英格蘭素瑞黑時，她的父親John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland26歲，母親Isabel le Bigod24歲。她於1253年與Robert de Veteripont在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1301年4月16日在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德沙普聖 瑪麗 馬格達萊也斯 艾比享年69歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭恩布利亞沙普聖 瑪麗 馬格達萊也斯 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: FitzJohn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name John , ‘son of John’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Fidgeon
meanings: 1  English: (i) variant of   Vivian  .  Compare  Videan  . (ii) variant of   Fitzjohn  .
2 Irish: reduced variant of Mag Guagáin, see McGuigan .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel FitzJohn was born about 1233, in Shere, Surrey, England, United Kingdom, her father, John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland, was 26 and her mother, Isabel le Bigod, was 24. She married Robert de Veteripont in 1253, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died on 16 April 1301, in St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 69, and was buried in St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Robert de Veteripont en 1253, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 avril 1301, à St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel FitzJohn um 1233 in Shere, Surrey, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland 26 und ihre Mutter Isabel le Bigod 24. Sie hat 1253 in Essex, England, United Kingdom Robert de Veteripont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 16. April 1301 im Alter von 69 Jahren in St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom und wurde in St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel FitzJohn è nata il circa 1233 nel Shere, Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Isabel le Bigod, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Robert de Veteripont nel 1253, nel Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 16 aprile 1301, nel St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolta a St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel FitzJohnが約1233年にイギリスイギリススリーシェアで生まれた時，父John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Irelandは26歳，母Isabel le Bigodは24歳であった。1253年にイギリスイギリスエセックスでRobert de Veteripontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1301年4月16日にイギリスイギリスウエストモーランドシャップスト マリー マグダリーネ'ス アビーで69歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスクウンブリアシャップスト マリー マグダリーネ'ス アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Cumbria: 영국 잉글랜드 쿠브리아 샤프 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 애브브이
ko.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 샤프 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Isabel FitzJohn(이)가 대략 1233년 영국 잉글랜드 수리 셔에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel le Bigod(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1253년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 Robert de Veteripont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1301년 4월 16일 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 샤프 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 애브브이에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 쿠브리아 샤프 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel FitzJohn nasceu aproximadamente 1233, em Shere, Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Isabel le Bigod, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert de Veteripont em 1253, em Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de abril de 1301, em St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel FitzJohn родилась около 1233 г. в местности шир, сурри, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel le Bigod, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Veteripont в 1253 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла 16 апреля 1301 г. в местности ст мэри магдеилине'с эбби, шап, вестморланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности ст мэри магдеилине'с эбби, шап, кумбря, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel FitzJohn nació aproximadamente 1233, en Shere, Surrey, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Isabel le Bigod, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Robert de Veteripont en 1253, en Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 16 de abril de 1301, en St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultada en St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
